principle: Do not request a video from the site until the generated file exists and has passed visual QA. Local ffmpeg fallbacks may be used only when clearly marked as temporary and not presented as Runway/Pika final output. Runway/Pika final exports replace local fallbacks only after visual QA proves they are more realistic, clearer, and more cinematic.

acceptance: Generated video must be visually better than the current keyframe before it replaces the page media source.
No text, logo, HUD, UI panel, dating metaphor, fake data, or fake brand mark inside the video.
The page must keep using the keyframe if the generated video is darker, blurrier, less real, or visually unstable.
After ingest, run npm run typecheck and npm run build before Chrome visual QA.

finalProductionQueue.chromeUploadRule: Use the user logged-in Runway/Pika Chrome session only when the site is stable on the image-to-video upload screen. Do not bypass login, payment, CAPTCHA, permission, or quota states.
finalProductionQueue.replacementRule: A Runway/Pika export can replace a local fallback only if it has no text, logo, HUD, UI, fake data, dating metaphor, face distortion, black frames, or severe blur, and if the first frame works as a poster.
finalProductionQueue.outputRule: Every accepted export must run through npm run media:ingest, then typecheck, build, and Chrome desktop QA.

latestReadyInput.nextAction: Enable Codex Chrome extension file URL access, then upload all eight V5 image-to-video inputs to Runway/Pika in priority order.

finalProductionQueue.nextAction: Generate Runway/Pika MP4 exports, place accepted files in output/runway-downloads/home-v5 with slug-based filenames, then run npm run media:ingest:batch -- --force --verify before Chrome desktop QA.

uploadHandoffPackage.reason: Chrome automation timed out while claiming Runway tab; all eight image-to-video inputs are packaged for manual upload or later retry.
uploadHandoffPackage.nextAction: Enable Chrome extension file URL access, then upload the eight inputs in queue order to Runway/Pika.

batchIngest.rule: Only visually accepted Runway/Pika exports may be placed in the downloads directory. The batch script ingests available files, then can run typecheck and build with --verify.
